description_long: This object is a double-barreled shotgun with a short, sawed-off design, featuring two parallel barrels and a dark wooden or synthetic stock. The barrels are side-by-side, and the receiver and trigger assembly are robust, with a visible break-action hinge beneath the barrels. The shotgun is accompanied by several red shotgun shells with brass bases, placed nearby. The stock is compact, lacking a full shoulder rest, and the grip is curved for handheld use. The metal surfaces show some wear and a matte finish, while the trigger guard is rounded and encloses two triggers. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian, close-quarters firearm suitable for urban environments.
description: A short, side-by-side double-barreled shotgun with a dark stock and several red shotgun shells. The compact design features a rounded trigger guard and visible break-action hinge.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin barrels and the compact grip, with shotgun shells visible beside the stock.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the side-by-side barrels, break-action hinge, and the arrangement of shotgun shells below.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the open ends of the barrels and the back of the receiver, with shells to the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile displaying the full length of the barrels, the grip, and the trigger guard.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, short barrel length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized, fully enclosing the triggers with no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
